text:
The drive note shifts during the burn — a subtle change in pitch that most people wouldn't notice. Your engineer does. She tilts her head, listening to something in the harmonics that you can't hear.

"It's fine," she says, before you ask. "Just... different. Every repair changes the voice a little. Like an instrument that's been restrung."

She goes back to her readouts. The drive hums on, slightly changed, carrying you forward.

choices.label: Ask her what she hears
choices.tone_note: Curiosity about the ship. Connection with crew.

effects.text: Listened to the engineer talk about the ship. She knows it better than anyone.

choices.label: Note it in the maintenance log
choices.tone_note: Professional. The captain who keeps records.

effects.text: Logged the drive harmonic shift. Small details matter.

choices.label: Let the moment pass
choices.tone_note: Silence between people who work together.

text:
The comms pick up system traffic as you transit — other ships going about their business. Two freighters arguing about lane priority. A patrol vessel running transponder checks and not finding what it wants. A mining shuttle requesting docking clearance with the bored efficiency of someone who does this every day.

The galaxy at work. You're passing through it.

choices.label: Listen for anything interesting
choices.tone_note: Paying attention to the background. Curiosity.

effects.text: Monitored system traffic. Nothing stood out — but knowing the rhythm matters.

choices.label: Filter it to background noise
choices.tone_note: Professional. Focus on your own heading.

text:
After the silence of transit, the station is overwhelming. Not loud — just full. Ventilation hum, distant conversation, the clank of cargo loaders, a child laughing somewhere several decks above. The air smells of recycled atmosphere with traces of cooking oil and machine lubricant.

Your ship's air was cleaner. This is dirtier, warmer, and unmistakably alive.

effects.text: Took a breath of station air. It tasted like civilization.

text:
Children watch your ship from behind a prefab barrier at the landing pad perimeter. Four of them, ranging from maybe six to twelve. The oldest has a datapad and is sketching your ship with the intense concentration of someone cataloguing something rare.

A younger one waves. It's the unselfconscious wave of someone who hasn't learned yet that not everyone waves back.

choices.label: Wave back
choices.tone_note: Small kindness. The galaxy doesn't always have to be hard.

effects.text: Waved at colony children watching the ship. Sometimes the small things.

choices.label: Tell the crew to secure the landing area
choices.tone_note: Safety first. Kids near a ship is a liability.

effects.text: Secured the landing perimeter. The children scattered.
